Brand & theme (theme palette, corners) are admin / theme-level settings that set brand character. Your view (font, density, colour mode) are the respondent's own runtime choices - System default font is always available. Nothing here is submitted.

System default is always available and uses your own device font.

How should we contact you?
Which topics may we contact you about? (Select all that apply)

Preview note: the colour-mode switcher, font picker and density control (under Your view) are the respondent's own runtime controls - density (Compact / Comfortable / Spacious, like Outlook or Gmail) re-spaces the chrome only and never changes the text-spacing floors or drops a target below 24px. Colour mode defaults to the operating system's prefers-color-scheme plus prefers-contrast: more, then persists the respondent's explicit choice. System default is always offered as a font so a respondent is never trapped in a shipped face (the accessibility escape hatch); the font list is a grouped picker (Accessibility / Popular / Playful & Kids / Traditional & Corporate). The theme and Corners controls (under Brand & theme) are admin / theme-level settings (issue #26): theme decides which palettes are available; Corners sets border-radius brand character. Every web font is embedded as a base64 woff2 data URI (OFL-1.1, except Roboto Apache-2.0) - the page makes no runtime network request; self-host the same woff2 in production.
